feed.title: SAP Community - SAP ERP
feed.subtitle: SAP ERP Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Document type field modification (BSART) in the purchase order
entry.content:
To modify the purchase order in transaction ME22N or the document type field (BSART) is being altered for purchase orders that are not in service category D.

For other purchase orders where the category field is not the service item it is being modified normally.
Please verify why purchase orders with category D Service Delivery are not altering the BSART field.

entry.title: CAPEX reporting for easy budget control
entry.content:
Hi all,

new to SAP here and it seems nothing is intuitive.

Trying to get a simple monthly CAPEX report, detailing the following for each asset:

  1. Cost Center
  2. Asset details - Cost Center, name, description, category (HW, project, machinery, etc.), Cost element if exist.
  3. Capitalization start + end date, or at least start date and capitalization period.
  4. Asset starting value, monthly depreciation amount, current book value by month
  5. Activated / not activated / expected activation date

I found some answers refering to S_ALR_87012060, others to purhcase additional SAP modules, one answer refering to SAC report (another module I can't use), and other answers which don't provide a simple solution.

Can such a simple business request, a minimum requirement by our budgeting team and function heads to better understand their cost structure, be achieved in SAP by a single transaction? or even a joining of several transactions?

Not using Hanas4.

Thank you

entry.title: VALUATION AREAS 01 AND 51 DO NOT ACCOUNT HOMOGENEOUSLY
entry.content:
When a new item is added to an already created asset account due to a purchase, the depreciation of the asset and the capital grant are consistent. In both cases, they depreciate the same amount, which is what the system should do. The problem identified arises when the addition is due to a fixed cash transfer using the ABUMN transaction. The depreciation amounts are not the same when they should be.

The conclusion is that both cases should be the same. However, they are not, and the correct calculation would be the one performed with the capital subsidy, not the accounting department.

The difference is that area 51 calculates the amortization based on the reference date and the 01 from the following month, but that happens because the asset is old (from previous years)

In this case the calculation is the same because it is based on the reference date, as it is within the fiscal year.

More information: As the system is configured, both depreciation areas must begin calculating depreciation the following month (as area 01 does). Indeed, it was determined that depreciation calculation begins the following month. However, in the case of a late asset acquisition, depreciation would also begin the following month, but taking into account the reference period indicated for the asset. Therefore, the calculation would include the months of delay from when the acquisition should have been made. For assets corresponding to unclosed fiscal years, areas 01 and 51 act uniformly, and in the opposite case, they do not, hence the discrepancy that should not occur.

entry.title: How to delete bgRFC units automatically?
entry.content:
Hi Team,

How to delete or Remove error units from bgRFC queue automatically without any Manual intervention.   

We know the process to delete this unit manually using SBGRFCMON however we would like to know any automated  process is available for this task.

Regards


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Sales order not approving for TAS item cat.
entry.content:
The user has created Purchase order for 1 line item without reference to the Purchase requisition no. and that too before approving the sales order. So now, the PR field in the schedule lines tab is showing blank for that line item and they are not able to approve the sales order. Also, the invoice and accounting document has been completed for this order.

Can someone please tell is there any way to approve this sales order because user is not agreeing to make a new order instead?

entry.title: I want to mass delete some old Purchase requistions but MASS not working
entry.content: I have tried using MASS to delete very old purchase requistions in SAP the MASS update says successful but it doesnt work. If i go back into MASS for the relevant PR i used for testing its unticked and also in table EBAN its not ticked. I have tried using ME57 but the closed option is greyed out. How do I get rid of all these old PR's there are hundreds of them. I can find nothing on this or any other community. Thanks Dean

entry.title: EBS configuration problem
entry.content:
I try to set up a FEban transaction. All the necessary settings in OT83 and OT59 were correctly set up, yet the system still selects the main GL account as the customer number, even though I have created strings for account number and account type. Please advise how to fix this.

Posting rules

Feban

ot59

strings

table febep

entry.title: Strategy Group 55: PIRs Consumption with reference to Sales Order Creation.
entry.content:
I'm working with Planned Independent Requirements (PIRs) linked to Characteristic/Configuration Data for Product Variants, and I need your insights to resolve a behavior I’ve encountered.

🎯 Objective:

Create PIRs for a product variant (Strategy Group 55) with assigned characteristics/configuration.

PIRs should only be consumed when a sales order with matching configuration is created.

PIRs should generate planned orders, but not convert to production orders until a matching sales order is received.

Lower-level components can be procured based on PIRs.

🧪 Test Scenario:

MRP View:

Configurable Material (Strategy 25): Individual/Collective = Individual

Product Variant (Strategy 55):

Individual/Collective = Collective

Assigned Configurable Material and completed characteristic assignment.

Maintained Planning Variant with characteristic values.

MD61: Created PIR for Product Variant.

MD04: PIR’s configuration was carried into the planned order, and lower-level components were planned correctly after MRP run.

⚠️ Issue / GAP Identified:
When I create a sales order with a different characteristic combination than the one in the PIR, the PIR is still being consumed - even though the configuration does not match. (other word, to be accurate 95% matches)

📌 Additional Info:

No Planning Table or Planning Profile used (per Strategy 55 behavior).

Assumption: PIR consumption is happening for closest match if no exact match is available.

Has anyone encountered this? Am I missing a key configuration or step to ensure that PIRs are only consumed for 100 % matching configurations?


Would appreciate any guidance or shared experiences!
Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Need help with figuring out technical name for "As of Date"
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community, 

We are trying to create a new report in SQ01. Customer has requested to add "As of Date" field as a selection category. We tried to find the technical name but failed. Can someone please let us know under which Table name and Technical name for "As of Date" comes. 

We tried with F1 - technical information but still no correct answers. It shows ZFBDT as technical name in table BSEG but when checked in SE16N it shows the Baseline date as ZFBDT. What are we missing here. 

Product Version: EHP8 - SAP ECC 6.0

entry.title: S/4 Hana conversion to margin analysis
entry.content:
Hi:

We upgraded from ECC 6.0 to S/4 Hana 2022 (on Rise) in August of 2023.  Our upgrade was just a technical upgrade, no new functionality.  We had/have costing based COPA active and want to look at turning on margin analysis now.  I am looking for any information from those who have made this conversion already.  We are trying to determine level of effort and what it will take to complete all the necessary evaluations. In my searching - I found documentation for those doing this as part of your S/4 migration but am not finding much on doing it stand alone after migration.

I would appreciate any guidance.

entry.title: HTTP Content Server all communication via SAP server?
entry.content:
Hi, 

we have a 3rd party ArchiveLink HTTP Content Server.

Create and Get request arrive at the HTTP Content Server from the end user client IP and not the SAP server IP

How can we configure SAP to have all communication going thru SAP server so the end user client doesn't need to communicate with the HTTP Content Server directly? (simpler firewall rules) 

We tried configure OAA3, OAG4,OAG1 and OAC0 but no luck. 
Please advise. 

OAC0 setting: 

Document Area: ArchiveLink
Storage type: HTTP Content Server

Document Area: ArchiveLink
Storage type: HTTP Content Server

entry.title: Purchase Requisition items not showing My Inbox Fiori
entry.content:
Dear SAP experts,

I'm implementing My Inbox after upgrading to SAP S4/HANA 2023.

I can't get the purchase requisitions items to appear in the app My Inbox 
I'm using Workflow WS20000077 with task TS20000159.

I implemented a note 3289039 - Missing item data in Purchase Requisition approval - SAP S/4HANA 2022 onward  and the following appears:

 

 

I maintain the settings before applying the note, and the header appears, but not the items.

 

I also followed the steps in this post.
Details of PR not showing on MY Fiori Inbox SAP S4/HANA 
Where I added and activated workflow WS20000077 in transaction SWETYPV.

And I checked task TS01800239 in SWFVISU

 

Thank you very much.
Regards,

entry.title: HU from EWM to ERP HU managed Sloc creates multiple record in VEKP and this lead to issues
entry.content:
HU is transfrerred from storage location managed in EWM to a storage location in ERP managed with HU and WM. in VEKP multiple records for same EXIDV are created. When GI is posted HU remains in status 0050 and when the HU is received in the HU managed storage location, a new record is created in VEKP. 

Standard transaction like LM18 reads the wrong record in VEKP

entry.title: HU not rehung to OD in ECC when ID is cancelled in EWM in a 2Step STO scenario
entry.content: We create with VLMOVE an outbound delivery in ECC from an HU-managed SLoc to a storage location in EWM (decentralized). For some reason we cancel the ID in EWM. The ID is cancelled in ECC as well but HU are not rehung to original OD

entry.title: Document Key (CA-DMS) definition - DIR and DRAW vs. KSSK-OBJEK
entry.content:
Hello,

How is the "Document Key" (CA-DMS) constructed ?

According to this old thread Unique Identifier from 2007, "Document Key" is apparently made by concatenating 4 values as per the following rule :

DOKAR (3 digits)
+ "modified DOKNR" (25 digits) ; a) if DOKNR is numerical, it is filled with 0's in front for a total of 25 numerical digits, or b) if DOKNR is text, space characters are added at the end to fill up to a total of 25 chars
+ DOKVR (2 digits)
+ DOKST (3 digits)


My questions :
1- Are the concatenation + front or back-filling of digits rules described above accurate and always valid ?
2- Where can I find the definition of the concatenation + digit filling rules in the official documentation from SAP ?
3- I need to do some external processing and link DIRs with Objects. I would like to read a table which has the "Document Key", DOKAR, DOKNR, DOKVR and DOKST . Does such a table exist ?

Thank you all in advance.

entry.title: Contract blocking error
entry.content:
The blocking error in the contract only occurs when there is a modification that affects the release strategy, and that causes the purchase document to be blocked (only in blocking, not release).
In this case, although internally the system "cleans" the fields that mirror the release strategy by changing the steps filled with an "X" and leaving them empty (""), externally, however, the code L (for released) does not change to B (for blocked), leading the user to believe that the document is still released, when in fact, the document is blocked.

Only by forcing a new lock does the document externally change the code L (released) to B (locked), therefore, we saw the need to open a ticket. We placed a document attached to the ticket, with images of two examples of purchasing documents that highlight the error for analysis. In this document, it is clear that internally SAP prepares the document for a new release, however, on the outside of the transaction, it remains as Released.

entry.title: login not possible on QAS system
entry.content:
Hi Team,

 

we renewed the license ERS and ASCS, we are getting this license error "login not possible error in license check".

entry.title: SAP MM : Request information and how to used field in purchase requisition table
entry.content:
Need information for field EPREFDOC and EPREFITM in purchase requisition table (EBAN)

  • What is it used for?
  • How to display field in PR screen?
  • Is it possible to used as free field for user input some information?

entry.title: I cannot maintain Form routine field in IS-REA partner
entry.content: I cannot maintain Form routine field in IS-REA partner in J7LE. This field is not editable
